This interface is designed for use with the Diamond pattern and focuses on the core functions needed for stable pool operations. Stable pools use the StableMath invariant which is optimized for assets that trade near parity (e.g., stablecoins). The amplification parameter controls the \"flatness\" of the curve - higher values (up to 50000) allow larger trades with lower slippage when prices are near parity.","kind":"dev","methods":{"getAmplificationParameter()":{"custom:selector":"0x6daccffa","custom:signature":"getAmplificationParameter()","details":"The amplification parameter may be in the process of updating. getAmplificationParameter().details: The amplification parameter may be in the process of updating. The returned value includes the precision multiplier (AMP_PRECISION = 1000).

returns.isUpdating: True if the amplification is currently transitioning.

returns.endValue: Ending amplification value for current/last transition.
returns.startValue: Starting amplification value for current/last transition.

getAmplificationState().notice: Get the full amplification state including transition parameters.
